title: Polymer clay textured bangle
subtitle: A flat-strip bangle shaped over a mandrel and baked rigid
excerpt: A polymer clay bangle built from a textured strip of clay shaped around a cylindrical mandrel and baked in that form. The baked bangle is rigid and lightweight. Intermediate difficulty because sizing and shaping require an accurate mandrel and careful joining.
sourceNotes: Polymer clay bangle construction derived from first principles.

glossaryTerms.definition: PVC-based modelling material that sets in a domestic oven at 110-130°C. Decorative use only.
The heat process that sets polymer clay in a domestic oven.

content.text: bangle is a flat strip shaped over a mandrel and baked rigid in that position. The mandrel can be a glass jar, a biscuit cutter, or a purpose-made acrylic bangle mandrel. The interior diameter must match the intended wrist size: measure the widest part of the hand (across the knuckles) and add 5-10 mm for slip-on clearance.

attrs.title: Baking polymer clay
attrs.body: Use a dedicated oven tray. Verify oven temperature with a thermometer. Do not share with food cookware.

items.name: Cylindrical mandrel (glass jar or biscuit cutter)
items.substitution: Any cylinder matching the wrist diameter

content.text: Measure the circumference of the mandrel. This becomes the inner circumference of the bangle. For a wrist that measures 16 cm (knuckle circumference), choose a mandrel with an outer circumference of about 17-18 cm. Wrap the mandrel in a strip of greaseproof paper: this prevents the clay from bonding to the glass and allows the baked bangle to slide off cleanly.
Roll the conditioned clay to 3 mm thickness. Cut a strip 15 mm wide and long enough to wrap the mandrel with a 5 mm overlap for joining.
Press a stamp or texture mat lightly into the flat strip before wrapping. Geometric patterns work well; organic leaf textures and lace both read clearly on a 15 mm band.

Wrap the strip around the mandrel, texture side outward. Overlap the ends by 5 mm and press firmly to join. The join is on the inside of the bangle and will not show when worn. Smooth the join with a finger. If the join is visible from outside, blend a very thin sliver of clay over it.
Place the mandrel on its side on the baking tray so the bangle hangs evenly. Bake at 110°C (fan) for 25 minutes.
on the mandrel holds the bangle's round form. Do not bake flat; the bangle will flatten.
Once cool, slide the bangle off the mandrel (the paper wrap helps here). Sand any rough edges. Apply acrylic gloss or matte varnish.

items.symptom: Bangle does not come off the mandrel after baking
items.cause: Paper wrap omitted, or clay bonded to the glass during baking
items.fix: Run a thin blade between clay and mandrel gently; always wrap the mandrel in greaseproof paper

items.cause: Join pressed onto the outer face
items.fix: Always position the join on the inside; blend with a thin clay sliver if already on the outside

items.symptom: Bangle is too small to slip over the hand
items.fix: Measure the knuckle circumference (not the wrist) and add at least 8 mm for slip-on clearance
